question_structure.context: A light rope of length $l$ has one end fixed at point $O$ and the other end attached to a small ball of mass $m$. The ball is thrown horizontally to the right from point $A$, which is at the same height as point $O$, with a certain initial velocity. After some time, the rope becomes taut, and thereafter, the ball swings in a vertical plane with $O$ as the center. It is known that the distance $OA$ is $\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}l$, and the angle between the rope and the vertical direction when the rope just becomes taut is $60^\circ$. The gravitational acceleration is $g$, and air resistance is negligible.
question_structure.sub_question_1: The velocity of the small ball when it is thrown and the velocity at the instant when the rope becomes taut; do not add any other content.
question_structure.sub_question_2: When the small ball swings to the lowest point, the magnitude of the tension force exerted by the string on the ball.

sub_question_1.step_1: After being thrown horizontally from point A until the string becomes taut, the ball undergoes projectile motion. At the instant the string becomes taut. In the horizontal direction, $L \sin 60° + \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} L = v_{0} t$
sub_question_1.step_3: The velocity at the instant before the rope becomes taut is $v = \sqrt{v_{0}^{2} + (g t)^{2}}$. The solution is $v_{0} = \sqrt{3 g L}$, $v = 2 \sqrt{g L}$.
sub_question_1.step_4: When the thin string is straightened, the angle $\alpha$ between the direction of velocity and the vertical direction. $\tan \alpha = \frac{v_{0}}{v_{y}} = \frac{\sqrt{3 g L}}{\sqrt{g L}} = \sqrt{3}$. Then $\alpha = 60°$
sub_question_1.step_5: The direction of the resultant velocity is along the direction of the thin rope, so the velocity of the ball after straightening is zero.

sub_question_2.step_6: When the pendulum reaches the lowest point, according to the law of conservation of mechanical energy, the gravitational potential energy is converted into kinetic energy. There is: $mg L (1 - \cos 60°) = \frac{1}{2} m v_{1}^{2}$
sub_question_2.step_7: The combined force of tension and gravity provides the centripetal force: $T - mg = \frac{m v_{1}^{2}}{L}$. The solution is $T = 2 mg$.

image_captions: A circle labeled 'A' is shown moving rightwards along a wavy horizontal line toward a point labeled 'O'. From 'O', a dotted line extends down and to the right at a 60-degree angle relative to a vertical dashed line. Another circle is positioned on this dotted line.

result_quantity.name: Horizontal displacement of the ball from the initial position to the moment the rope becomes taut
Vertical displacement of the ball from the initial position to the moment the rope becomes taut

step_3.physical_theorem: Kinematics of projectile motion and Solving simultaneous equations

result_quantity.name: Initial velocity of the ball when thrown
Velocity of the ball at the instant before the rope becomes taut
Angle between the direction of velocity and the vertical direction at the instant before the rope becomes taut
Velocity of the ball after the rope becomes taut

step_6.physical_theorem: Law of conservation of mechanical energy

result_quantity.name: Velocity of the ball at the lowest point

step_7.physical_theorem: Newton's second law and Centripetal force

result_quantity.name: Tension force exerted by the string on the ball at the lowest point
